description: This set of figures presents true stress-true strain curves from uniaxial tensile tests of three aged Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al low-density steel samples (AN750-T, AN800-T, AN850-T), comparing experimental measurements (red solid lines) with prediction results from the iso-work constitutive model (grey dashed lines). The shaded area between the two curves represents back stress (σ_B) induced work hardening, which increases with rising true strain. Inset digital image correlation (DIC) strain field maps show varying strain distribution characteristics: AN750-T exhibits relatively uniform strain and stress relieving during plastic deformation; AN800-T shows secondary local strain concentration that propagates at 45° to cause necking; AN850-T presents relatively uniform strain distribution at early deformation. The maximum σ_B values at necking strain are 217 MPa, 300 MPa, 278 MPa for the three samples, respectively. These results confirm that coordinated deformation between soft and hard domains, back stress hardening, and TRIP effect synergistically improve the ductility of the heterogeneous multiphase steel, mitigating premature failure caused by stress concentration.
